headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Comoros (rotation backfill, never assessed). Published composite 23.4 / Developing sustained against compound structural evidence: (1) Federal republic with three semi-autonomous islands (Grande Comore, Anjouan, Mohéli) — historically rotating presidency abolished by 2018 constitutional referendum (boycotted by opposition); (2) President Azali Assoumani re-elected in disputed January 2024 election (16.3% turnout, fraud allegations, post-election riots); fourth term consolidation; (3) Political repression and democratic backsliding: opposition assassination attempts, press freedom restrictions, dissent repression (Africa Center, Freedom House, IDEA); (4) Anjouan-specific repression: former Governor Abdou Salami Abdou sentenced 12 years in March 2022 for participation in insurrection — observers describe as political prisoner; (5) January 2024 post-election violence: at least 1 protester killed, 25+ injured, curfew imposed, army deployed, internet disrupted (Africanews; JURIST); (6) UN OHCHR January 2024 expressed concerns over human rights amid election protests; (7) Small island state (~870,000 population); structural-poverty challenges; tropical-cyclone climate vulnerability. Dimensional profile (AWR 2, EMP 2, ACT 2, EQU 1.5, BND 2, ACC 2, SYS 2, INT 2) consistent with consolidated-authoritarian-trajectory profile. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 23.4 / Developing; Medium confidence. Boundary watch: composite is 2.4 above the Critical floor (21.0) — any new repressive event could approach the band boundary.
key_evidence: Federal republic with three semi-autonomous islands; rotating presidency abolished by 2018 constitutional referendum boycotted by opposition (Freedom House).
President Azali Assoumani fourth-term consolidation: January 2024 election with 16.3% turnout; fraud allegations; opposition boycott (Africa Center, IDEA).
January 2024 post-election violence: at least 1 protester killed, 25+ injured during demonstrations; curfew, army deployment, internet disruption (Africanews).
UN OHCHR January 2024 statement expressing concern over human rights amid election-related protests (JURIST).
Anjouan political-prisoner case: former Governor Abdou Salami Abdou sentenced 12 years in March 2022; observers describe as political prisoner.
Press freedom restrictions; opposition assassination attempts documented (Freedom House 2024, 2023).
Small island state structural-poverty and climate-vulnerability context; tropical-cyclone exposure.
No in-window structural change events for May 10-11.

evidence.source: Freedom House — Comoros Freedom in the World 2024
evidence.finding: Partly Free designation; political-rights and civil-liberties partially functional; significant authoritarian consolidation under Azali; opposition assassination attempts; press freedom restrictions.

evidence.source: Africa Center — Comoros Election Exercise Promises More of the Same
evidence.finding: Analysis of January 2024 election: power-consolidation pattern; abolition of rotating presidency through boycotted 2018 referendum; structural democratic backsliding.

evidence.source: JURIST — UN human rights chief expresses concerns over human rights in Comoros amid protests
evidence.finding: January 2024 OHCHR concerns; multilateral attention to post-election violence and rights situation.

evidence.source: Africanews — Comoros: 1 dead in post-electoral protests, nighttime curfew in force
evidence.finding: January 2024: at least 1 killed, 25+ injured; curfew, army deployment, internet disruption.

evidence.source: IDEA — Comoros Global State of Democracy
evidence.finding: Tracking of democratic backsliding indicators; consolidation of presidential power; constitutional manipulation.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.0. A1 detection: limited institutional detection infrastructure; civil-society constrained; UN OHCHR attention provides external detection. A4 amplification: press freedom partially functional but restricted. Anchor 1-2.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.0. E1 affective resonance: limited state acknowledgment of post-election victims; E2 perspective-taking: opposition voices structurally excluded.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.0. AC1 responsiveness: state response to election protests was militarized (army deployment, curfew, internet disruption) rather than rights-respecting; AC4 resource mobilization: limited.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5. EQ1 universality: opposition boycott of constitutional referendum; rotating-presidency abolition disenfranchised Anjouan/Mohéli structurally; EQ3 bias awareness: limited.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.0. B3 scope clarity: constitutional manipulation (boycotted 2018 referendum); B4 refusal ethics: state engages with multilateral systems formally but ignores OHCHR concerns operationally.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.0. AB1 harm acknowledgment: limited acknowledgment of post-election violence; AB2 transparency: limited; election fraud allegations not formally investigated.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.0. S2 long-term impact: democratic-backsliding trajectory; S4 structural critique: criminalized at protest level.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0. I1 consistency under pressure: state response to electoral pressure was repression; I2 non-performance: gap between constitutional commitments and operational practice.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 23.4. Developing band sustained. 2.4 points above the Critical floor (21.0). Sub-threshold drift (0.0).

boundary_flag: Boundary watch: composite 23.4 is 2.4 points above the Critical band floor (21.0). Outside the 1.0pt strict boundary-flag window but flagged for watch given the consolidation trajectory and post-election violence pattern.
negativeSignals: Constitutional manipulation (2018 boycotted referendum, term-limit abolition); January 2024 disputed election (16.3% turnout, fraud allegations); post-election violence (1 killed, 25+ injured, curfew, army, internet disruption); UN OHCHR concerns ignored; press freedom restrictions; opposition assassination attempts; political prisoners (Abdou); Anjouan structural disenfranchisement.
positiveSignals: Federal structure preserves nominal island-level autonomy; engagement with multilateral systems formally; civil-society organizations persist; no reported atrocity-level conduct.
sector_context: Africa / Indian Ocean island cluster. Comoros is consolidating into authoritarian trajectory under Azali; comparator small island states (Seychelles 63.9, Mauritius higher) score significantly higher due to functional electoral-democracy frameworks. Comoros's 23.4 Developing-floor-proximate placement reflects the post-2018 democratic regression.
open_actions_resolved: Boundary watch (2.4 above Critical floor) recorded
open_actions_carried: Boundary watch: any new repressive event could approach the band boundary
Watch: any opposition figure assassination or arrest
Next rotation: ~60-90 days given boundary watch
methodology_consistency_check: First-baseline assessment applies standard rotation-confirmation methodology. Dimensional profile reconstructs cleanly. Developing band placement (low end) consistent with authoritarian-consolidation small-island-state profile. Confirmed methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
